title: [phase:critique] [family:ui-runtime-review] [provider_hint:subagent] Review the current Happy production resolution. Facts: production /happy/ serves updated HTML with Live Session wired to /cockpit; /cockpit returns 200; unauthenticated GET /api/happy/state,/events,/task-detail return 401 by design...
summary_text: **Safest Sequence (Prod Smoke: reset-password only)** 1. Preflight lock + snapshot. - Confirm target is exactly `happy-smoke-operator` and no other operator is using it. - Snapshot current row + refresh token count for rollback (`email`, `role`, `is_active`, `password_hash`, `reset_token`, `reset_token_expires`, `refresh_count`). - Abort if user row is missing. 2. Prepare temporary reset state (single short window). - Generate one-time `reset_token` (high entropy) and short expiry (5-10 min max). - Set user to reset-eligible: `is_active=1`, `reset_token=<token>`, `reset_token_expires=<now+ttl>`. - Seed one known refresh token row for this user (sentinel) so revocation can be proven. 3. Execute reset-password API. - Call `POST /api/cockpit/auth/reset-password` with `{ resetToken, newPassword }`. - Expect `200` + new `accessToken` and `refreshToken`. (Endpoint behavior confirms active+token check and revocation+new pair issuance: [cockpit-api.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts):695, [cockpit-api.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts):714) 4. Prove revocation + login success. - Try `POST /api/cockpit/auth/refresh` with the seeded old refresh token; expect reject (`401`). - Login with new password via `POST /api/cockpit/auth/login`; expect `200`. - Optional: refresh using the new login refresh token once to verify normal flow. 5. Mandatory cleanup (always run in `finally`). - `DELETE FROM cockpit_refresh_tokens WHERE user_id='happy-smoke-operator'` - `UPDATE cockpit_users SET is_active=0, password_hash=NULL, reset_token=NULL, reset_token_expires=NULL WHERE user_id='happy-smoke-operator'` - Verify post-state: `is_active=0`, `password_hash IS NULL`, `reset_token IS NULL`, `reset_token_expires IS NULL`, `COUNT(refresh_tokens)=0`. **Concrete Guardrails** - Use a unique run-id and log every step with explicit pass/fail gates. - Hard timeout + guaranteed cleanup path (`trap/finally`) even on API failure. - Never print reset token or passwords in logs. - Keep reset token TTL short; do not reuse tokens. - Run as single-user maintenance window for that smoke account to avoid race with other tests. - Expect auth endpoint rate limits (`/reset-password`, `/login`, `/refresh`) and fail cleanly on `429`. **Likely Missed Cleanup Points** - Forgetting `reset_token_expires=NULL` (clearing token alone is not enough). - Deleting only the seeded refresh token, not newly issued ones from reset/login/refresh checks. - Leaving `is_active=1` if run aborts mid-flight. - Not restoring snapshot fields if they differed from expected baseline (especially `email`/`role` if your setup touched them). - Ignoring residual short-lived access tokens (cannot be server-revoked immediately in current design); acceptable only because this is a dedicated smoke user.
